item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it weird if I think Big Charlie is actually sort of cute? It's the big eyes that do it for me.

Ugly cuteness aside, I do like the idea of a Lovecraftian abomination that is really just an ordinary animal… it's the people around it who believe it is some sort of eldritch god. All Big Charlie wants to do is eat and sleep - it's everybody else who's been worshipping him.

+1 for cute Cthulhu cow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just being thorough. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the three perspectives for what it's worth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the big eyes and pointy smile. Pretty sure it wasn't intentional though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this at all similar to Momo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The face reminds me of Momo and thought this was momo inspired

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding those before me about the image. Also, the dialogue in the interviews is still pretty unrealistic and awkward, which is a concern I raised when critting the draft.

Gonna be a reluctant -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No one's compared it to 682. If any, you bringing up the comparison to 682 or any other regenerative and immortal SCPs is more likely to make people compare it to that as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. The picture doesn't really fit in an article. I'm fine with making articles about certain images, but this one in particular is just not right for it.

The image doesn't fit at all and I think the best course for this would be to remove the picture entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just love coming back to this post every so often to see what people think of it. I for one like this post and please IF YOUR GOING TO COMPARE IT TO 682 SHUT UP AS REGENERATION OR IMMORTALITY ARE COMMON THINGS TO SEE IN THE SCP WORLD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was just playing around to see if I could make the image more realistic and send it to the author, but it has so many problems. I'm not even an artist, but I can see why it's so hard to fix:

-the proportions on the creature aren't appropriate for a vertebrate of its height. Not in the spooky sense, in the 'it looks like you just stretched out an image of a little creature'. The legs are way too skinny in proportion to the body, and the neck is too small in relation to the head.
-the shading/lighting doesn't match the lighting of the rest of the picture, making it look, again, like it's photoshopped in.
-the hair and eye are cartoonish, both in size and in coloration. The only creature that size with eyes that big is the giant squid.

I found this out when blurring the image just made it worse, emphasizing the above problems.

This SCP entry was designed around this picture, and a few dozen people like it, but it's just not good for a picture that's supposed to be 'real'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, but around five notable authors have given criticism to the article's image. I'd assume that they'd be a little more forgiving had the image been edited to make it look better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah. I didn't really notice how weird that was. I was thinking like they tested it on him eventually but that doesn't really make sense. Thanks for pointing that out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, quick plothole right here:

But a couple of days later we realized that he had grown back most of what we cut off him. We cut off some more and then tried feeding it to Barney and he didn't even notice it tasting different.

And this:

It was nine years ago.

Those don't add with this:

I've been working there for about 4 years and they and already had him when I started working there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally am not saying that that's not why it's positively rated, I'm just noting that it was promoted on Henderson's twitter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe it has a positive rating because people like it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is because of the usage of a well-known artist's artwork used in an SCP here.

The SCP was also promoted on Trevor's twitter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A lot of the criticisms for the article always mention the image here. Images in articles set up a first impression on the reader; if you have a good image, there's a good chance that readers won't be taken out of their immersion and it won't stretch their disbelief. Author, I think it would benefit this piece greatly if you either:

  • Edited the image into something that looks realistic for its setting.
  • Change the image into something else. Ask Trevor Henderson if he could make some adjustments or if he could make a new one altogether.
  • Remove the image completely.

These are just suggestions on the image itself since it appears to be the one that's the most distracting and jarring of all the criticisms mentioned above.

ETA: Also, this may sound accusatory but it seems that the only reason why the article has quite a high rating (in my eyes, at least) is because of the usage of a well-known artist's artwork used in an SCP here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how you added it in the comments was great. Just wanted to make sure. I see why he approved it though, I love this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, here are the reasons as to why I had downvoted this. I will leave this simple and sweet as I am sick with a cold. This will be done in order of the first reason as to why I downvoted to nitpicks. Sorry if a paragraph doesn't make sense I'm rn on cough meds to make this evil cold go away.

+ Crit
- Crit

0. Conceptually it's boring, it's just a monster that produces infinite meat. I have no qualms with a boring anomaly but, it's just not executed well enough for me to be intrigued.

1. The image looks super fake.

2. Interview one adds nothing and can be cut out. The beginning of interview 3 and interview 2 are repeating the exact same thing. While it is only a short section, it is still repeated and unneeded info. The interview logs are treated as an exposition dump for the SCP with a poorly executed exploration into each individual characters and their relationship with it. You need to flesh these people out more. Why does the butcherer worship it? You don't just follow something that extreme for no reason. Add in more of an element of insanity like maybe have the butcher claim it performed miracles.

3. Have you ever been around a cattle rancher? Cause I have, my grandfather, my horse trainer, my vet, and our hay supplier are all cattle workers and I'll tell you that they would've have gotten a vet immediately as this is, more than likely, a $3,0001 cow that they are just letting die or they are letting their entire herd get sick or are allowing a deformity that kills both the mother and calf to go loose.
Also, a nitpick, people who are ranchers use their hunting rifle to off the ill/dying calf so, we just call it a gun.

4. This isn't the entire reason I downvoted just a small thing to point out. There are minor things in the con pro that are somewhat annoying.
"8 meter by 10-meter cell" Does it have to be this size? Will it breach containment if not put in this exact sized room? Also, you need a third measurement.

"Constant monitoring is not necessary, though SCP-4158 must be checked at a minimum of three times a day" The following can be removed from the latter statement" Constant monitoring is not necessary."

5. The discovery should be marked separately from the description.

6. " Calls to animal control were made by multiple residents about a large cow with mange roaming by Highway 17." This looks nothing like a cow if I were to see it, I would say, "It looks like a titan from that one anime."

ETA 2.0: So I had forgotten about 1 really small, but yet big detail. Every single cow, and meat gotten from a cow, is marked by the FDA. Essentially, what this means is that if you really wanted to, you can track down the exact place that meat came from. ie. The stranger would've had to have some form of identification for the prenegnate cow and the slaughter house would've had the meat marked during packaging. This is another weird nitpick but, it’s apart of the FDA’s code due to mad-cow/ prion disorders affecting cattle and humans. This could be fixed by the stranger faking papers and the dudes using the deceased cattle’s identification code


ETA: Added in a collapsible
ETA 2: added in a weird nitpick I just remembered
Footnotes
1. Cattle can be that amount. Most times it is $10 per lbs. on a 300 lbs. cow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thank you for putting into words what i couldn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just isn't working for me overall.

I'm not as averse to "growing meat cow" as the other commenters, because I think that could possibly make some pretty sweet imagery (see: Fatberg). Like this line here.

Testing was halted after SCP-4158 began showing evidence of neural tissue generation.

However, the picture immediately breaks immersion for me, because it clearly looks like photoshop or an artwork. And then you've got three interviews that don't pay their rent for the space they're taking up: by that I mean that they don't tell us anything new about the anomaly except that the one dude worships it. And you could have told us that in maybe… 3 lines, total.

It just feels like unused potential, with too much filler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the read. And thanks for actually telling me what you didn't like about it. I hate when people don't because there's no way for me to improve if I don't know what's wrong

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All this really has going for it is the slight "ew" face I made when I realized that these guys were using this cow's meat in their packaging. Other than that, this just feels like 682 with less of an underlying "the world will end" plot device. The image pulled me directly out of immersion, it just looks as though it's photoshopped into a background. I'll give it to you that you have some very effective imagery in this, but the interview logs don't particularly make this piece for me; the man "worshipping" the cow just felt like an unnecessary way to put gore into this and directly spoiled the damn good horror of these guys packing this cow's meat over and over again.

Maybe some edits here and there would make this better to read, but honestly it just doesn't really work for me. You could try reading up about the horrors of the meat processing during the Gilded Age and draw some inspiration from that; I'd like to see a bit more horror in that aspect than "he worship the cow!! ew!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I've updated and improved the interview logs quite a bit. It would be great if you could give it another read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also downvoted because of the image. The consistent use of realistic images really helps me remain immersed in the stories. and this pulls me right out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you are allowed to make any changes you want, it’s your article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It won't get you in trouble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Replacing the first two interviews with a letter on how to care for him could be a good idea but I'm worried that would be changing it too much for it already being posted would get me in trouble

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…cows that were in the possession of the Butcher's Block Slaughterhouse at the time were all administrated Class E amnestics.

Good idea, wouldn't want those cows spreading the secret.

Could use touching-up in a few places but otherwise well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the third definitely needs some work still, the religious aspect could be subtly hinted to in the video as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that the third needs to be fixed up a little as well since the worship aspect just came out of nowhere, but I think the video would be an excellent way to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I talked to the author a bit about the interviews in PMs, but after sleeping on this and feeling slightly less sick, I feel like the simplest method is to get rid of the first two, and replace it with a staff training tape for how to care for it and where it came from. If I were to help rewrite this (which I also briefly proposed), that's the main change I would make.

Having interviews explain everything comes off as too direct, but having some sort of medium that you need to infer stuff from what the person is saying (i.e. a training tape) due to the lack of knowledge of the field/background of what is being discussed makes it feel less direct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really vague and doesn't give the author anything to work off of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well what don't you like about it? I want to know so that I can improve it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good, but I think it could benefit from a rewrite.
Still worthy of a +1.
EDIT:The main problem is the interviews, they're really clunky and just feel off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if we just ask him to join the wiki?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it's really that easy for someone to get Trevor's blessing, i may have to try it myself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I was thinking, I feel like this image by Trevor of the same thing would work a lot better here, it feels a bit more real. The author even has that image downloaded to this page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. This isn't that good, but it has a lot of potential. I'd hate to see Trevor's art go to waste. I'd love to see a rewrite of this.
edit: I retract my statement. This is actually pretty alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice thingmajig, (kinda spoiled considering how I follow trevor’s Twitter, but oh well)
I just feel that the interviews are quite awkward, with theese two lines being especially egregious:

Interviewer: Let the record show that Mr. Mossman nodded his head in confirmation.

Interviewer: Okay. Let's start the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As much as I like Trevor's art and would want to use it in an article, this image in particular is too… Unrealistic for me to believe that.

item.title: Big Charlie
item.content:encoded: Congratulations on completing your SCP! The one main issue I saw with your article has been fixed. Only small errors remain. Once again congrats!

item.title: Re: Big Charlie The Growing Cow
item.content:encoded: I like this post and I cant wait to see it grow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just take a screenshot of his blessing and post it here.

item.content:encoded: Its alright it happens.

item.title: (no title)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HOLY SHIT I cant believe i forgot to do that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey author! Your image needs attribution to show that it's CC-compliant or that you have permission to use it.

item.title: Big Charlie The Growing Cow
item.content:encoded:
This is my first real published SCP! I got good reviews from The Butterfly Squad and everyone else I had read it so I hope it turned out good!

I was inspired by the piece Big Charlie by Trevor Henderson so I got his permission and wrote this, so special thanks to him.

Since this is my first SCP, if it doesn't go over well I'll take it down and rewrite it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That image is from Trevor Henderson. I don't see any citation or credit to him- wanted to make sure you had permission/left appropriate credit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Woops everywhere i look its SCP-XXXX you may want to fix that.
